svg.aria-label: The loop detector. A stuck controller re-issues the identical action search_policy with the argument discount code for ORD-9999, every step returning no discount code on file. A detector keyed on action plus args counts repeats: step 1 count x1, step 2 count x2, step 3 count x3. At the threshold of 3 it flags LOOP DETECTED and signals the circuit breaker, which trips and returns a graceful partial result after 3 steps with the meter at steps 3, tokens 240, dollars 0.0024, about 3.6 seconds, well under budget because the loop caught it first. An annotation notes this formalizes RAG Part 19's informal saw-the-same-search-twice note.
svg.title: The loop detector: count identical (action, args), flag a loop at the threshold, signal the breaker

svg.text: The loop detector: the same action, three times, is a stuck agent

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: Key on (action, args). Count exact repeats. At the threshold, flag a loop and signal the breaker. Re-running the same search cannot help.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: THE STUCK CONTROLLER RE-ISSUES THE IDENTICAL CALL EVERY STEP

svg.text: Why the loop caught it, not the budget

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: At 3 steps the meter is well under every ceiling (4 steps, 600 tokens, $0.02, 12s). The cheaper, faster guard wins.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif
Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: P19 said informally: "if the agent runs the same search twice, stop." Here it is a reusable, threshold-driven check.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Exact repeats are the simple case. Alternating A-B-A-B cycles are a natural extension; this detector keys on the exact (action, args) tuple.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Wall-clock is simulated (~1.2s per step, not measured); cost is an illustrative per-token estimate, not a real price sheet.
